title: US Economy Shrinks: Why Mortgage Rates ArenвАЩt Dropping (Yet)
summary: The US economy is shrinking, with GDP declining this quarter. WeвАЩre getting closer to recession territory, so why arenвАЩt mortgage rates dropping? WeвАЩll explain how one crucial part of the economy is staying strongвАФkeeping the Fed from cutting and delaying the typical rate-drop that comes with a recession. WhatвАЩs stopping us from going back to sub-6% mortgage rates? WeвАЩll break it down in this episode. The economy is changingвАФfast. The US saw its GDP turn negative last quarter as many Americans braced for the impact of tariffs. But even with the overall economy lagging, labor data remains strong. Jobs are still being created, unemployment is relatively low, and Americans are going to work. This may be the single factor keeping the Fed in limbo, unable to cut rates any further. So, what happens if the labor market breaks? Home builders were already anxious over the past year, and now theyвАЩre getting even more hesitant to build. With tariffs pushing up prices for materials, building (and buying) a house could get much more expensive. And with builders already dropping prices, could this lead to a broader decline in home prices across the nation? In This Episode We Cover A worrying sign for the US economy and whether it could trigger lower mortgage rates The one thing standing in the way of the Fed finally cutting rates again Tariff effects on GDP and the first signs of what they could do to our economy New labor market numbers and why jobs are being added as the economy shrinks Are we in a recession? And does it even matter if we are? And So Much More! title: Financial Freedom in 6 Years by Buying Rentals with Just $6,000 Down
summary: This investor turned $6,000 into financial freedom in just six years. He did it in a major market and became a millionaire by age 28 simply by repeating this beginner-friendly rental property strategy over and over again. And, even though he started earlier, you can STILL buy properties like his, at affordable prices, that cash flow, in the same market today. Where is he investing, and how did he scale up so fast? WeвАЩre breaking it all down in todayвАЩs episode. Jeremy Taggart saved every dollar from his college internship, knowing he wanted to invest in real estate after graduation. He bought his first house, a small multifamily, for just $6,000 down, lived in it, did some DIY renovations, and increased the value. Thanks to the rent savings, he bought another property the following yearвАФthis time, making $50,000 (tax-free!) by fixing it up. This was just the start of the вАЬrinse and repeatвАЭ strategy that would turn Jeremy into a millionaire before he was thirty. But it wasnвАЩt easy. Jeremy was fired from his job, had to start working for himself, and did what many real estate investors wonвАЩt. The result? Complete financial independence less than a decade after graduating college. His strategy still works in 2025, but will you use it? In This Episode We Cover: The easiest way to buy your first property with low money down (only $6,000!) Making $50,000 tax-free using the вАЬBRRRR strategyвАЭ (buy, rehab, rent, refinance, repeat) The affordable, cash-flowing, stable real estate market Jeremy invests in (you can, too!) Is becoming a real estate agent worth it as a real estate investor? Why Jeremy switched from вАЬcash flowвАЭ to вАЬequityвАЭ investing for long-term wealth And So Much More! title: #308: Darwin Under the Microscope - Will 2025 Mark the Return of Property Growth for AustraliaвАЩs Most Affordable & Smallest Capital City?
podcast_name: The Property Trio (formerly The Property Planner, Buyer and Professor)
summary: Got a question for the trio? https://forms.zohopublic.com/propertyplanningaustralia/form/GotaquestionforthePropertyTrio/formperma/zYCQAxzE_24CVlDafP1ozyzwtmB-8m1iCNtCTgDvHXM рЯОЩпЄПThis episode was inspired by a thoughtful message from a listener who challenged us to dig deeper into DarwinвАЩs property market after we referenced it briefly on a previous show. Some investment advisors are touting Darwin as their top pick for capital growth in 2025, so Mike takes the reins to explore if DarwinвАЩs time in the spotlight is finally back. рЯП† Why Darwin, and Why Now? Michael, one of our listeners, shared compelling on-the-ground insights about a surge in investor and buyersвАЩ agent activity. From off-market sales and investor loans soaring in the NT, to Buyer's Agents reportedly purchasing 20+ properties per weekвАФDarwin's market seems to be heating up fast. Cate reflects on the risks of artificial price uplifts when too many Buyers Agents flood a small market and shares her cautious optimism. Yes, Darwin has clocked over 1% monthly growth in both March and April, but as she reminds usвАФtwo data points donвАЩt make a trend line. рЯТђ Investor Buzz Recent growth figures are starting to align with Michael's anecdotal evidence. Darwin may finally be rebounding after an 11-year slumpвАФlonger than any other capital. But is it investor-fuelled, or is there something broader taking place in Darwin? рЯУК Data, History, and Economic Context Dave offers a reality check with stats: Darwin housing values are still 4.9% below 2012 levels. Despite high rental yields (the best in the nation), long-term growth has lagged, and the marketвАЩs small size adds volatility. Cate and Dave also explore the city's unique profile: Population: ~150,000Heavy reliance on public sector employmentMining = 30% of NT's revenueHome ownership below national averageGovernment stimulus and generous first-home buyer grantsрЯУЙ Risk or Reward? DarwinвАЩs rental yields and affordability are attractive, but economic diversity, investor saturation, and project delivery are key concerns. Cate shares the practical challenges of property upkeep from afar, while Dave reflects on the pitfalls of yield-chasing strategies. рЯФЃ Will Darwin Shine in 2025? The Trio agrees: Darwin has had remarkable highs (second-highest median house price in the late 90s and early 2010s) and harsh lows. With strong early signals and renewed investor interest, it may be poised for a comebackвАФbut sustainability is the real test. ¬† рЯОІ Tune in now for a balanced, data-backed, and researched discussion on whether Darwin is worth your investor attention in 2025. Shownotes: https://www.propertytrio.com.au/2025/05/05/listener-questions-darwin/

title: Why Behavioural Economics Matters in Real Estate with Evan Lucas
podcast_name: The Elephant In The Room Property Podcast | Inside Australian Real Estate
summary: What if the real driver behind AustraliaвАЩs property obsession isnвАЩt logic, but human behaviour?  In this episode, weвАЩre joined by behavioural economist Evan Lucas, and letвАЩs just sayвА¶ this oneвАЩs a thinker. We talk about how our attitudes toward property have shifted since the pandemic; whatвАЩs stuck, whatвАЩs changed, and why the DIY mindset, generational habits, and even the Bunnings effect all feed into the way we view real estate. ItвАЩs not just about shelter. ItвАЩs about control. Identity. Security. And that deeply emotional idea of вАЬhome.вАЭ  Evan pulls back the curtain on how weвАЩre wired to respond to urgency, pressure, and even shiny marketing tactics, and how those biases can lead us straight into the wrong property decisions (even when we think weвАЩre being smart about it). We get into pain points like home bias, dopamine hits, and the big one: why losing money on property feels so personal, because for many of us, it is.  ThereвАЩs so much in here for buyers, sellers, and anyone trying to make sense of todayвАЩs market. If youвАЩve ever wondered why the numbers donвАЩt always add up to the decisions people make, this episode explains a lot.  Episode Highlights:  00:00 - Introduction  01:06 - Who is Evan Lucas?  02:44 - Why Australians are so obsessed with property  06:07 - The real consequences of a property-driven culture  09:17 - Why does behavioural economics matter so much when youвАЩre investing?  13:31 - When did Evan realise behaviour was driving investment outcomes¬†  22:38 - The behavioural biases that quietly sabotage property buyers  29:31 - The big differences in beliefs and behaviours around property across generations  34:38 - Why housing has become a generational and cultural tug-of-war  38:28 - What COVID changed and what it didnвАЩt  42:29 - Will the regional shift last now that offices are calling people back?  46:29 - Work-life tug-of-war: WhoвАЩs really in control now?  51:13 - Evan's thoughts on allowing first home buyers to access their super  57:12 - What Evan thinks about value and what it really means in the context of property  1:03:16 - Evan LucasвАЩ property dumbo  About Our Guest:  Evan Lucas is a leading behavioural economist, author, and financial commentator known for making complex money matters relatable and human. With a background in global finance and a passion for understanding how emotions and biases shape financial decisions, Evan helps Australians rethink the way they engage with money, investing, and property. He regularly appears in national media and is the author of Mind Over Money, where he explores why logic often takes a back seat when it comes to personal finance.
